items.front: CPDLC: what seven categories does it augment for voice communication?
items.back: Altitudes, routes, speeds, holding clearances, altimeters, advisories, and frequency changes. CPDLC augments — does not replace — voice. Per 14-2-1(a).
The sector team is responsible for sending and responding to CPDLC messages. Per 14-2-1(b).

items.front: CPDLC: when should its use be minimized?
items.back: During critical phases of flight. Per 14-2-1(c).

items.front: CPDLC: immediate or expeditious clearances rule
items.back: Should NOT be used to issue immediate or expeditious clearances unless voice communication is not operationally feasible. Per 14-2-1(d).

items.front: CPDLC: handling when aircraft exits continental U.S.
items.back: Unless otherwise coordinated, last controller must terminate CPDLC connection upon transfer to non-U.S. facility or ATOP sector. Coordinate with eligibility sector before terminating from another position. Per 14-2-1(g-h).
Follow FAA Order JO 7110.65, Chapter 10, Emergencies. Per 14-2-1(i).
Use of automated VCI during CPDLC operations complies with 2-1-17, Radio Communications. Per 14-2-1(f).

items.front: CPDLC: when may abnormal indications be acknowledged?
items.back: Only after required coordination has been performed. Per 14-2-2(b).

items.front: CPDLC override phraseology and restatement rule
items.back: DISREGARD CPDLC (type) CLEARANCE (description) AND SEND AN UNABLE (alternate clearance). If multi-element, restate ENTIRE clearance. Per 14-2-2(c).

items.front: CPDLC: why must pilot send UNABLE when override?
items.back: Canceling an uplink only removes it from the ground system; the uplink remains open on the flight deck. Pilot must send UNABLE to close it on flight deck. Also, the CPDLC clearance may not have been received yet. Per 14-2-2(c-d) notes.

items.front: CPDLC: NORDO aircraft with active CPDLC connection
items.back: Sector with eligibility may mark aircraft on frequency to allow CPDLC communications. Use 5-2-4 (Radio Failure) and 10-4-4 (Communications Failure) for aircraft with two-way voice failure. Per 14-2-2(e).

items.front: CPDLC: when automated altimeter settings are sent
items.back: Only in response to a monitor transfer of communication (TOC), or an altitude uplink when the assigned altitude is below FL180. Per 14-2-3(a) note.

items.front: CPDLC: unexpected connection loss with no prior voice
items.back: Controller must ensure voice communication is established and maintained with that aircraft. Per 14-2-3(b).

Broadcast: 'Attention all aircraft; CPDLC no longer in use. Do not downlink any messages until further advised.' Resolve any open or abnormally closed uplinks by voice with each aircraft. Per 14-2-3(c).
Control instructions and messages needing acknowledgement must NOT use advisory/free text. Abbreviations must comply with JO 7340.2 (Contractions). Per 14-2-4(a).
At/above FL390, WILCO satisfies 5-7-2(b) concurrence. Must NOT use CPDLC for speed adjustments on routes/procedures with published speed restrictions. Per 14-2-4(b).

Must NOT use CPDLC to clear aircraft out of holding (route uplink lacks clearance limit). Must NOT use CPDLC for holding instructions if aircraft has climb/descend via. Cancel vertical nav portion first. Per 14-2-4(c).
